theory.intro: A table whose $x$-values go up by $1$ can be written as a rule $y=mx+c$. The coefficient $m$ is how much $y$ changes each time $x$ increases by $1$, and the constant $c$ is the $y$-value in the column where $x=0$.
facts: $m$ is the change in $y$ as $x$ increases by $1$; a falling $y$-row makes $m$ negative.
$c$ is the $y$-value in the column where $x=0$.
When $c=0$ the line passes through the origin, so the rule is just $y=mx$.
The rule is correct only if it reproduces the listed $y$-value in every column.
steps: Find m: the change in y as x increases by 1.
Find c: the y-value where x = 0.
Write y = mx + c and check every column.

foundation.question_text: Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&4&6&8&10\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&0&3&6&9\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&-1&3&7&11\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&-1&0&1&2\\\hline y&2&5&8&11\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&7&8&9&10\end{array}$.

development.question_text: Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&9&6&3&0\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&-2&-1&0&1\\\hline y&8&5&2&-1\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&0&1&2&3\\\hline y&-2&-3&-4&-5\end{array}$.
Find the equation for the table $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&-1&0&1&2\\\hline y&1&-3&-7&-11\end{array}$.

mastery.question_text: The columns are not in order. Find the equation for $\begin{array}{c|cccc}x&2&0&3&1\\\hline y&1&7&-2&4\end{array}$.
The columns are not in order. Find the equation for $\begin{array}{c|ccccc}x&3&0&-1&2&1\\\hline y&7&1&-1&5&3\end{array}$.
